title: Rei Eva 01
wikitext:
[]: Rei/Eva-01             Quote Post by Zuggy » Sun Jan 27, 2008 5:24 pm I was taking a couple of screenshots for the Rei Ayanami article and noticed something I'd meant to post here for ages - In episode 4 Ritsuko performs specturm scan on Rei using lasers. and it looks like this on her computer: The rainbow colours are similar to the wave-pattern scan of Shamshel that Ritsuko shows Misato and Shinji. The red marks I believe indicate the injuries that Rei sustained during the first activation test. The markings are identical to those on the skin of Eva-01: Why the same markings? We know that Eva-01 has blue hair from End of Evangelion and then there's that famous illustration of Eva-01 with blue hair and boobs. Eva-01 is the "only true offspring of Lilith" so there is a big connection between Eva-01 and Rei already - but what are those little circles with lines going through them about? Do they mark chi meridians (possibly of relevance for metaphysical biology and ATF manipulation) or something else entirely?

[]:             Quote Post by Dr. Nick » Sun Jan 27, 2008 6:05 pm It's Applegeeks viral marketing.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Sun Jan 27, 2008 6:25 pm Interesting. I'm not seeing it though. Elaborate a bit more please.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Sun Jan 27, 2008 6:33 pm It does not appear so: that's just how they animated medical scans, and there appears to be no relation between these two things whatsoever. The red marks cannot possibly represent Rei's injuries: they're just part of the rainbow color band which is vertically repeated across the entire display of her body, and further they do not really correspond to her injuries.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Jan 27, 2008 6:39 pm I'm not sure why Zuggy said "red marks" for Rei; typo, I'm guessing. He's referring to the tiny white circles with the lines coming out -- see Dr. Nick's link.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Sun Jan 27, 2008 6:51 pm Well, two of them are red on Rei. The fact that they're on both of them is probably just because the company that made the machines and the company that distributed the Evas are the same. Nerv. It wouldn't be surprising that it had the same "power on" symbol. Or I'm just too tired to think straight. @Dr. N's link: I like the "Power On" boxers. It's so perfect.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sun Jan 27, 2008 7:04 pm I like this. It's nice. BTW, was Unit 01's hair supposed to be "flaming" at the end of EoE or did the blueness of the hair just make it look that way?

[]:             Quote Post by AsukaChan » Sun Jan 27, 2008 7:07 pm I noticed the little Sachiel-face shape on Unit-01's head before I noticed the markings. XD The markings on Rei remind me of all the little things on the plugsuit. Like the little knobs and stuff. I'm probably wrong. (:

[]:             Quote Post by V » Sun Jan 27, 2008 9:15 pm Reichu wrote: I'm not sure why Zuggy said "red marks" for Rei; typo, I'm guessing. He's referring to the tiny white circles with the lines coming out -- see Dr. Nick's link.

[]:             Quote Post by Sceleris » Mon Jan 28, 2008 4:41 am ?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Jan 28, 2008 4:49 am I thought about that, but there are far more circle-things in the scan than actual electrodes attached to Rei. I'm not sure there is an exact placement correspondence, either.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Mon Jan 28, 2008 5:01 am It's not, the dot on her shoulder is not as close to her neck as opposed to the electrode.

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Mon Jan 28, 2008 5:38 am Reichu wrote: I'm not sure why Zuggy said "red marks" for Rei; typo, I'm guessing. He's referring to the tiny white circles with the lines coming out -- see Dr. Nick's link.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Mon Jan 28, 2008 5:41 am Isn't it just the bandages?

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Mon Jan 28, 2008 5:48 am Probably, but I thought it was worth mentioning.

[]:             Quote Post by Semisubtle » Mon Jan 28, 2008 3:59 pm I think this might be diving a bit TOO deep here. If there was a connection, I'm sure there'd be more evidence than this.

[]:             Quote Post by Dr. Nick » Wed Jan 30, 2008 8:17 am If I may attempt to craft an explanation that satisfies Occam's Razor: the circles mark spots of some (medical) importance. The lines coming out of them are text, probably describing what the spots mark. They apparently don't have any in-story significance.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Wed Jan 30, 2008 9:28 am Semisubtle wrote: I think this might be diving a bit TOO deep here. If there was a connection, I'm sure there'd be more evidence than this.
